reviews.review_description:
The current navigation UI is "Press thumbstick to teleport."  This is a difficult UI to use: you have to press the thumbstick down, and then, while holding it down, move left/right to change the teleport angle.  And, this doesn't follow Oculus app conventions -- most apps teleport by pushing the thumbstick forward (which is my preference); a few use the A button.

Since the whole point of this app is to move about the apartment, I suggest improving teleport UI at the very least.  I'd also suggest having slide be an option (though not the default, since it can cause motion sickness).  For a good example, see Walkabout Mini Golf, which allows both.

(This is an update of my previous unnecessarily rude review.)

developer_response.body: I really appreciate the updated review, and I wasn't at all offended with the previous one. You are 100% correct and I also don't like this UX. It's just, I didn't have the time to update it. Will do it

reviews.review_description: I couldn't really do anything in this app I couldn't move forward/ back/side to side..I get only spin around 360 and  change the decorations from one theme to another!!

developer_response.body: Its a walkthrough experience where you can only move around and change the style. Did you try using the thumbstick to move?

reviews.review_description: It's not incredible, but still quite good, probably the best VR home on Quest. I wish there were more on this device, like the old days of Viveport.

developer_response.body: Thank you. It's just a commercial demo. We're going to publish more soon

reviews.review_description: Excellent graphics. Well worth the download.
I would like to change the house up in more detail we need a inventory of decorations and appliances

developer_response.body: Thank you for the feedback. It is basically a promotional demo, and you can reach out to us if you need to get your apartment in VR like this. We do build more customization features as well

reviews.review_description: A good demo at showing the possibility of using VR to introduce previews of interior designs. A must-see for real estate industry agencies.

Really shows the ability of the Quest to produce some great looking scenes when optimized for the platform (Anne Frank VR also did some amazing looking interior stuff). The performance was a bit rough for a few seconds upon loading and upon movement upstairs, but overall it was pretty good. Can definitely see the potential in this as a commercial tool.
please add more content like this. I can pay for this.

developer_response.body: Thank you, really appreciate your review. We'll publish more demos

reviews.review_description: I liked the app. Great decorating ideas. I wish there were more style options and I wish you could pick different furniture and personalize the home as you wish.

There are a few typo:
- at first, you have to use the right controller as the left controller cannot interact with changing the furniture.
- There is a sign upstairs saying to press A with the left controller... well A is on the right controller.
Really great quality demo of what can be achieved in ArchVis applications. I strive to get the same quality from my own work. Well done.
